paper_title: Cohort Study of Diatomite Plant Workers

methods.description: This method involves identifying the cohort of workers from a diatomite plant and collecting relevant employment and health data to study cancer incidence.

steps.description: The cohort comprised workers from a diatomite plant and those who worked at loading the finished product onto ships, identified from employment records from 1967 to 1981.

Each worker's personal identification number was established from the name, date of birth, home address, or a previous identification number and compared with the National Register and the Register of Deceased Persons.
Records supplied information on employment years, hours worked each year, task, and occupation.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing exposure levels to respirable cristobalite and linking health examination records to cancer registry data.

steps.description: Records of personal samples of respirable cristobalite showed variation according to a person's type of work and location within the factory.
Records were linked by means of the personal identification number of each subject to the cancer register.
steps.searchable_summary: Link health records to cancer registry

methods.description: This method calculates the number of expected cancer cases based on person-years and specific cancer incidence rates.

steps.description: Person-years were calculated for each subject starting with the first year on the employment records and ending with the date of death or 1 December 1991.
The number of expected cancers was calculated based on the number of person-years for each 5-year age category during the study period, multiplied by the specific incidence of cancer site.
